dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8951 IH: Zero Tolerance for Fraudsters Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Calvert (for himself, Mr. Owens, and Mr. Hunt) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To establish mandatory minimum terms of imprisonment for fraud offenses, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Zero Tolerance for Fraudsters Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: Chapter 47 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Mandatory minimum sentences for certain offenses
section.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, in the case of an offense under section 1001, 1002, 1003, 1010, 1012, 1031, 1035, or 1040, the punishment shall be—

paragraph.text: if the offense involved an amount of $1,000,000 or more, but less than $5,000,000, a fine under this title, and imprisonment for not less than one year and not more than 10 years; or
if the offense involved an amount of $5,000,000 or more, a fine under this title, and imprisonment for not less than 5 years and not more than 20 years,

quoted-block.quoted-block-continuation-text: except in the case that the punishment under such section provides for a greater term of imprisonment.

paragraph.text: The table of sections for chapter 47 of title 18, United States Code is amended by inserting after the item related to 1040 the following:

toc.toc-entry: 1041. Mandatory minimum sentences for certain offenses.

subsection.header: Mail fraud and other fraud offenses

paragraph.text: Chapter 63 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Mandatory minimum sentences for certain offenses
section.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, in the case of an offense under section 1341, 1343, 1345, or 1347, the punishment shall be—

paragraph.text: if the offense involved an amount of $1,000,000 or more, but less than $5,000,000, a fine under this title, and imprisonment for not less than one year and not more than 10 years; or
if the offense involved an amount of $5,000,000 or more, a fine under this title, and imprisonment for not less than 5 years and not more than 20 years,

quoted-block.quoted-block-continuation-text: except in the case that the punishment under such section provides for a greater term of imprisonment.

paragraph.text: The table of sections for chapter 47 of title 18, United States Code is amended by inserting after the item related to 1352 the following:

toc.toc-entry: 1353. Mandatory minimum sentences for certain offenses.
